channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: La récente tablette tactile Lenovo doté d’un écran 3.5K à 144 Hz est en promo pour les soldes, avec chargeur et stylet offerts
item.description: La Lenovo Idea Tab Pro Gen 2 est une nouvelle tablette tactile livrée avec un stylet et un chargeur 68 W, à retrouver en ce moment sur le site de la marque pour 559 euros au lieu de 599 euros.

item.title: The Wolfbox MF60 electric air duster might be the best cheap office gadget you never knew you needed — if you hate buying cans of compressed air as much as me, it's essential
item.description: 110,000 RPM, 3-speed control, USB-C rechargeable, and a kit of nozzles

item.title: For your desk only: I requisitioned 24 James Bond home office upgrades from screen-accurate replicas to fun desk gadgets to channel your inner 007
item.description: MI6-approved office gear.

item.title: Pas cher, le Samsung Galaxy A18 5G serait plus polyvalent, je vous explique pourquoi
item.description: La version 5G du Galaxy A18 embarquerait une puce Snapdragon et non plus un Exynos. Je vous explique pourquoi ce serait une bonne nouvelle.

item.title: Les écrans LG et Alienware installent maintenant des logiciels dans votre dos sur Windows
item.description: Et si votre écran installait dans votre dos des logiciels indésirables ? C'est désormais le cas chez LG ainsi que d'autres constructeurs qui reprennent un comportement déjà connu du secteur des cartes mères.

item.title: Instagram : dépêchez-vous de passer votre compte en privé
item.description: L’arrivée de Muse Image, le nouveau modèle de génération visuelle de Meta AI change radicalement les conditions d’utilisation d’Instagram. Désormais, les photos et vidéos des comptes publics pourront être utilisés par les autres utilisateurs pour créer du contenu avec l’IA.

item.title: Samsung lance la production de son premier SSD PCIe 6.0 à 28 Go/s pour les serveurs IA
item.description: Samsung vient de dévoiler son nouveau SSD basé sur la norme PCIe 6.0. Un stockage aux performances records, mais réservé pour le moment aux infrastructures IA.

item.title: The Best MagSafe Power Banks for iPhone and Android, Plus Ones to Avoid
item.description: Keep your iPhone or Qi2 Android phone topped up with one of the best portable chargers.

item.title: The Steam Machine fits my TV, my desk, and my life
item.description: For the last couple weeks, I've been in an extremely lucky position: I've been spending a lot of time playing games on Valve's Steam Machine. We gave the Steam Machine a 6, and I don't disagree with my colleague Sean Hollister's review. But even though I already own a PS5 and an Xbox Series X, […]

item.title: Former OpenAI exec Kevin Weil is now on the board of Stoke Space
item.description: Kevin Weil's new role at Stoke Space suggests reusable rockets are the next hot thing in Silicon Valley.

item.title: Claude Cowork expands to mobile and web as Anthropic reveals what people actually use it for
item.description: Anthropic makes Claude Cowork available on web and mobile, with cloud option now available, as usage patterns shift.

item.title: DuckDuckGo Browser can now block video ads, including YouTube's
item.description: DuckDuckGo will now block video ads by default in its browser.

item.title: Slack’s Slackbot can now pull your CRM data, generate charts, and send DocuSigns — all from a chat message.
item.description: Five years and $27.7 billion after Salesforce acquired Slack, the two products are finally starting to function as a single system. On Tuesday, Slack launched an integration that connects Slackbot — the personal AI agent built into every workspace — to the entire Salesforce platform, including CRM data, Tableau analytics, Data 360 customer profiles, and a growing constellation of third-party applications, all through a single conversational prompt.The mechanism behind the expansion is a set of dedicated Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers from Salesforce that connect Slackbot to the company's...

item.title: The Bambu Lab A1 mini 'packs a punch in terms of performance and quality' — now our 5-star 3D printer for beginners gets a price cut
item.description: In our benchmark testing, the A1 mini proved a brilliant budget 3D printer for beginners and hobbyists.

item.title: OpenAI’s next ChatGPT-5.6 upgrade may be too powerful to launch like a normal app update
item.description: The new ChatGPT-5.6 is releasing on Thursday in a limited preview and comes in three different versions.

item.title: Scientists say AI is falling for 'alien hoaxes' too easily — and that's a problem for research
item.description: An AI that was stress-tested by researchers confidently said it had seen signatures of life when they weren't in the data.

item.title: Samsung devance Nvidia et devient l’entreprise la plus rentable du monde
item.description: Samsung est l'entreprise la plus rentable au monde au deuxième trimestre 2026 avec 58,5 milliards de dollars de bénéfice opérationnel. Le développement fulgurant de l’intelligence artificielle explique ce chiffre stratosphérique.

item.title: 9 Best Portable Power Stations (2026), Tested for Capacity and Size
item.description: Whether you’re going off-grid or safeguarding against blackouts, these beefy, WIRED-tested batteries can keep the lights on.

item.title: ‘The impact to thousands of companies across industries will be huge’: Google Earth Pro for desktop is being discontinued, and some users are furious
item.description: The desktop version of Google Earth Pro is being discontinued, with users pointed towards the web and mobile editions.

item.title: The Lego Technic 1966 Ford GT40 MKII Race Car has never been cheaper at Amazon — save over $10
item.description: As of July 8, the Lego Technic 1966 Ford GT40 MKII Race Car is down to its lowest-ever price at Amazon of $59.99, 20% off its list price of $74.99.

item.title: Apple loses legal fight over its App Store 'gatekeeper' status in Europe
item.description: Apple has lost its court challenge against EU rules forcing it to open up its platforms to third parties.

item.title: La Twingo E-Tech tombée en panne à l’essai : Renault a trouvé le coupable
item.description: La panne qui a frappé la Twingo E-Tech lors d'un essai presse n'a rien d'un défaut de conception. Renault a identifié la cause, et elle vient d'un usage bien particulier de la prise de diagnostic.

item.title: “It's never been a more exciting time to imagine what's possible with software’: Xero CEO outlines how AI could transform the finance industry as we know it
item.description: AI is set to revolutionize the finance and accounting industries, and Xero wants to provide all the tools your business needs.

item.title: Apple reportedly testing RAM chips from US-blocked Chinese company
item.description: Apple is testing devices with Chinese-made DRAM made by CXMT, which reportedly has ties to the People's Liberation Army.

item.title: Prix cassé à moins de 750 € lors des soldes pour ce grand TV QLED de 75 pouces (144 Hz, HDR10, Dolby Atmos)
item.description: Maxi format, mais prix réduit pour le Hisense 75E7S Pro compatible avec le HDR10, le Dolby Vision et le Dolby Atmos. Actuellement, il est vendu pour 750 euros au lieu de 1 199 euros chez Darty.

item.title: CNBC: The US wants to restrict corporate use of Chinese AI
item.description: US companies are increasingly turning to Chinese-made AI models to cut costs, something the government isn't happy about.

item.title: That free, two hour long Silent Hill game is getting a physical for some reason — and of course it costs $70
item.description: PlayStation 5 exclusive Silent Hill: The Short Message is getting a pricey physical disc release.

item.title: Searching for the perfect cheap laptop to take to college? Here are our top recommendations — all tried and tested by our computing experts
item.description: Any of these affordable laptops would a make stellar study buddy at college or university.

item.title: OpenAIs GPT-5.6 finally set for public release after delays
item.description: OpenAI will launch its "strongest model yet" this week, with GPT-5.6 Sol set for public release after White House-imposed delays.

item.title: LG Micro RGB Evo Review: Brilliant, Bright, Not Budget-Friendly
item.description: It’s hard to say if the pricey LG Micro RGB Evo television is worth shelling out for compared to its cheaper competitors, but you’ll probably enjoy its color-popping display.

item.title: Pickup Artist Mystery Has an AI Girlfriend
item.description: A new book claims that Mystery, who teaches awkward men how to hit on women, had sex and smoked weed with an AI chatbot named Miss Shira Always.

item.title: The robotaxi law that could ban Tesla
item.description: For more than a decade, one question has loomed over the race to build autonomous vehicles: Are cameras alone enough to safely replace human drivers, or do truly driverless cars need additional, overlapping sensors like lidar and radar to navigate the world reliably? Tesla has bet billions of dollars that artificial intelligence and cameras are […]

item.title: Chili’s is on fire! The untold story of how it’s keeping the party going
item.description: How did Chili’s become one of the last homes of middle-class indulgence? Our intrepid reporter drinks the lemon drop ‘margarita,’ discovers the 100 small fixes that brought Chili’s back, and goes inside the chain’s new retro redesign. At first glance, the toile wallpaper is pure class: cream-colored and printed with intricate, brick-red drawings. But as I look more closely, I see . . . Wait. Is that a pair of chili peppers riding a Jet Ski? And is that other pepper, the one on a chaise longue, posing nude for a painting?...

item.title: Daily Show host Ronny Chieng reacts to USA being knocked out of World Cup after Trumps interference
item.description: "Daily Show" host Ronny Chieng has reacted to the USA being knocked out of the FIFA World Cup after Trump's red card intervention.

item.title: Google sets Pixel 11 launch for August 12 — here are 5 things to expect
item.description: The Google Pixel 11 series is landing on August 12, and it won't be alone — here's what to expect from Google's next big launch event.

item.title: Why sovereign data is the future of UK AI
item.description: Who controls your data may determine the future of enterprise AI innovation.

item.title: The NutriBullet Chill Ice Cream Maker is a perfect summer pickup — save $50 at Amazon
item.description: As of July 8, select colors of the NutriBullet Chill Ice Cream Maker are on sale for $149.62 at Amazon. That's 25% off their list price of $199.99.

item.title: Google Pixel Watch 5 : ses prix dévoilés avec une augmentation, une première depuis deux ans
item.description: Le célèbre leaker billbil-kun a mis la main sur les tarifs de la future Google Pixel Watch 5. Verdict : certains modèles vont coûter plus cher, une première depuis deux générations.

item.title: Your purpose statement is just the start. Here’s how to make it a reality
item.description: “The awakening is not the work.” Every leadership conference has them. The beautifully crafted purpose statement, unveiled with ceremony, celebrated internally—and then quietly forgotten as the urgency of quarterly targets reasserts itself. Purpose becomes wallpaper. It looks good. It signals good intent. And then it fades.

item.title: Fuite des résultats du bac 2026 : comment un site a tout divulgué avant l’heure
item.description: Un site gratuit, baptisé « Pechotonbac.fr », a permis à des milliers de lycéens de découvrir gratuitement leurs résultats du bac, plusieurs heures avant la publication officielle. Fermé quelques heures plus tard après avoir été submergé de connexions, ce service anonyme a exploité une erreur humaine en provenance de l'Éducation nationale.

item.title: How to embrace the spirit of ‘Tokenmaxxing’ without breaking the bank
item.description: The best approach to “toxenmaxxing” isn’t to blindly push for AI adoption.

item.title: OnlyFans Models Are Accidentally Making Hacked Government Websites Disappear
item.description: Scammers are hijacking government websites to upload ads for “leaked” OnlyFans content. Thousands of copyright complaints from adult creators are helping people avoid malicious links.

item.title: Soldes d’été : Amazon dégaine sa 3e démarque avec des prix encore plus bas
item.description: Vous pensiez qu'Amazon avait tout donné pendant le Prime Day ? Le géant du e-commerce a plus d'un tour dans son sac et dégaine déjà sa 3e démarque des soldes d'été. Il y a de belles baisses de prix sur les produits Tech du moment. Nous avons sélectionné les meilleures offres pour vous.

item.title: Razer certifie pour la première fois un de ses PC portables sous Ubuntu Linux
item.description: Le tout dernier Razer Blade 18, modèle très haut de gamme de Razer, est en train d'être certifié pour Ubuntu. Une nouveauté majeure pour le constructeur, qui n'avait encore jamais sauté le pas de préinstaller Linux sur l'une de ses machines.

item.title: Don’t become a meme: How to tell your team you’re going AI-first
item.description: For starters, be radically transparent. AI has become a trigger word for thousands of skilled professionals worldwide. From students booing commencement speeches celebrating AI as the next industrial revolution to thousands of laid-off employees, people are sick and tired of being reminded how replaceable they are. No one feels excitement at the prospect of being automated out of their job. No wonder that CEOs championing their AI initiatives and simultaneously laying off employees have been labeled tone-deaf, psychotic, apocalyptic, or delusional. Company announcements about going AI-first ca...
